mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate reduces the risk-free discount rate, directly lifting equity valuations and producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the subsequent month.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate falls below 8.0%, it signals a tightening labor market that boosts consumer spending and corporate earnings, driving positive equity returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: SPX forward returns remain negative or flat when UNRATE crosses below 8.0% in more than 60% of observed instances.

mechanism_narrative: Strong industrial production growth combined with rising retail sales indicates synchronized demand expansion, which historically precedes earnings upgrades and positive S&P 500 returns over the next month.
failure_condition: The joint signal produces negative or zero predictive power when both series rise simultaneously over a 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate exceeds 0.25% while core inflation remains sticky, the market prices in prolonged tightening, which suppresses equity multiples and yields negative forward returns.
failure_condition: Forward returns are positive in more than 50% of instances where FEDFUNDS crosses above 0.25% with sticky inflation.

mechanism_narrative: A sequential increase in new housing starts reflects improving business and consumer confidence, which translates into higher corporate revenue expectations and positive S&P 500 returns over the next month.
failure_condition: HOUST increases do not correlate with positive 20-day SPX returns over any 3-year period.

mechanism_narrative: When nonfarm payrolls exceed 200,000 for two consecutive months, it confirms robust economic momentum, reducing recession fears and driving positive equity returns over the subsequent quarter.
candidate_trigger: PAYEMS monthly change > 200000 for 2 consecutive months
failure_condition: SPX forward returns are negative or flat in more than 55% of instances following two consecutive months of strong payroll growth.

mechanism_narrative: Divergence between rising industrial production and falling unemployment indicates supply-side bottlenecks that compress profit margins, leading to negative S&P 500 returns over the next month.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to predict negative returns when INDPRO rises and UNRATE falls simultaneously over a 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Positive quarterly GDP growth accelerates earnings revisions, which directly lifts equity valuations over the next 60 trading days regardless of short-term volatility.
failure_condition: Positive GDPC1 growth quarters do not produce statistically significant positive 60-day forward returns over any 3-year period.
